en: Let me sing for my beloved my love song concerning his vineyard: My beloved had a vineyard on a very fertile hill.
zh: 我要為我所愛的唱歌， 這歌是關於他的葡萄園： 我所愛的人有一個葡萄園，在肥美的山岡上。

en: He dug it and cleared it of stones, and planted it with choice vines; he built a watchtower in the midst of it, and hewed out a wine vat in it; and he looked for it to yield grapes, but it yielded wild grapes.
zh: 他把園子的泥土挖鬆了，撿去石頭， 栽種了上好的葡萄樹； 在園中建造了一座守望樓， 又鑿了一個榨酒池。 他期望結出好葡萄， 卻結出野葡萄。

en: And now, O inhabitants of Jerusalem and men of Judah, judge between me and my vineyard.
zh: 耶路撒冷的居民和猶大人哪！ 現在請你們在我與我的葡萄園之間斷定是非吧！

en: What more was there to do for my vineyard, that I have not done in it? When I looked for it to yield grapes, why did it yield wild grapes?
zh: 我為我的葡萄園所作的以外，還有甚麼要作的呢？ 我期望它結出好葡萄，它為甚麼倒結出野葡萄呢？

en: And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard. I will remove its hedge, and it shall be devoured; I will break down its wall, and it shall be trampled down.
zh: 現在我告訴你們，我要怎樣處理我的葡萄園： 我要把它的籬笆撤去，使它被吞滅； 我要把它的圍牆拆毀，使它被踐踏。

en: I will make it a waste; it shall not be pruned or hoed, and briers and thorns shall grow up; I will also command the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.
zh: 我要使它荒廢， 不再修剪，也不再耕耘， 荊棘和蒺藜卻要長起來； 我也要吩咐雲不再降雨在園子上。

en: For the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah are his pleasant planting; and he looked for justice, but behold, bloodshed; for righteousness, but behold, an outcry!
zh: 因為萬軍之耶和華的葡萄園就是以色列家， 他喜悅的樹就是猶大人； 他期望的是公平，但看到的只是流血的事； 他期望的是公義，聽到的只是哀叫聲。

en: Woe to those who join house to house, who add field to field, until there is no more room, and you are made to dwell alone in the midst of the land.
zh: 那些使房屋連接房屋，使田地連接田地， 以致不留餘地的人，有禍了！ 你們只可以獨居在境內。

en: The LORD of hosts has sworn in my hearing: "Surely many houses shall be desolate, large and beautiful houses, without inhabitant.
zh: 我聽聞萬軍之耶和華說：“必有許多房屋變成荒涼， 甚至那些又大又美的房屋也沒有人居住。

en: For ten acres of vineyard shall yield but one bath, and a homer of seed shall yield but an ephah."
zh: 十公頃葡萄園只出二十二公升酒； 二百二十公升穀種只結二十二公升糧食。”

en: Woe to those who rise early in the morning, that they may run after strong drink, who tarry late into the evening as wine inflames them!
zh: 那些清早起來，追求濃酒， 留連到晚上，以致因酒發燒的人，有禍了！

en: They have lyre and harp, tambourine and flute and wine at their feasts, but they do not regard the deeds of the LORD, or see the work of his hands.
zh: 在他們的筵席上，有琴、瑟、手鼓、笛和酒， 但他們不理耶和華的作為， 也不留心他手所作的。

en: Therefore my people go into exile for lack of knowledge; their honored men go hungry, and their multitude is parched with thirst.
zh: 所以我的人民因無知被擄去； 他們的尊貴人十分飢餓； 他們的群眾極其乾渴。

en: Therefore Sheol has enlarged its appetite and opened its mouth beyond measure, and the nobility of Jerusalem and her multitude will go down, her revelers and he who exults in her.
zh: 故此，陰間擴張它的咽喉，張大它的口，沒有限量； 耶路撒冷城的榮耀 、群眾、喧嚷的人和在城中作樂的人，都要下到陰間。

en: Man is humbled, and each one is brought low, and the eyes of the haughty are brought low.
zh: 卑賤人俯首，尊貴人降卑， 眼目高傲的也降卑。

en: But the LORD of hosts is exalted in justice, and the Holy God shows himself holy in righteousness.
zh: 唯獨萬軍之耶和華，因公平被高舉； 至聖的 神，因公義顯為聖。

en: Then shall the lambs graze as in their pasture, and nomads shall eat among the ruins of the rich.
zh: 那時羊羔必來吃草，像在自己的草場一樣； 富裕者的荒場被寄居者隨意吃用。

en: Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood, who draw sin as with cart ropes,
zh: 那些人虛假的繩索牽引罪孽， 又像用車繩拉罪惡的人，有禍了！

en: who say: "Let him be quick, let him speed his work that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near, and let it come, that we may know it!"
zh: 他們說：“願他趕快、迅速地成就他的作為，給我們看看！ 願以色列的聖者所計劃的臨近！ 願它來到，好讓我們知道！”

en: Woe to those who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness, who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter!
zh: 那些稱惡為善，稱善為惡， 以暗為光，以光為暗， 以苦為甜，以甜為苦的人，有禍了！

en: Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes, and shrewd in their own sight!
zh: 那些自以為有智慧， 自視為聰明的人，有禍了！

en: Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine, and valiant men in mixing strong drink,
zh: 那些勇於喝酒， 又精於調和濃酒的人，有禍了！

en: who acquit the guilty for a bribe, and deprive the innocent of his right!
zh: 他們因受了賄賂，就稱惡人為義； 卻把義人的權益奪去。

en: Therefore, as the tongue of fire devours the stubble, and as dry grass sinks down in the flame, so their root will be as rottenness, and their blossom go up like dust; for they have rejected the law of the LORD of hosts, and have despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.
zh: 因此，火舌怎樣吞滅碎稭， 乾草怎樣落在火燄之中， 照樣，他們的根必像腐朽之物，他們的花必像塵土飛揚； 因為他們棄絕萬軍之耶和華的訓誨， 藐視以色列聖者的話。

en: Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against his people, and he stretched out his hand against them and struck them, and the mountains quaked; and their corpses were as refuse in the midst of the streets. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
zh: 所以耶和華的怒氣向他的子民發作， 他伸出手來擊打他們， 群山都震動，他們的屍體在街上好像糞土， 雖然這樣，他的怒氣還未轉消， 他的手仍然伸出。

en: He will raise a signal for nations afar off, and whistle for them from the ends of the earth; and behold, quickly, speedily they come!
zh: 他必豎立旗幟，招一國的人從遠方而來， 發出哨聲把他們從地極召來； 看！他們必急速快奔而來。

en: None is weary, none stumbles, none slumbers or sleeps, not a waistband is loose, not a sandal strap broken;
zh: 他們當中沒有疲倦的，沒有絆倒的， 沒有打盹的，也沒有睡覺的； 他們的腰帶沒有放鬆， 鞋帶也沒有折斷。

en: their arrows are sharp, all their bows bent, their horses' hoofs seem like flint, and their wheels like the whirlwind.
zh: 他們的箭銳利，所有的弓都上了弦； 他們的馬蹄看來像火石，車輛似旋風。

en: Their roaring is like a lion, like young lions they roar; they growl and seize their prey; they carry it off, and none can rescue.
zh: 他們的吼叫像母獅，他們的咆哮如幼獅； 他們咆哮，攫取獵物， 把它叼去，無人援救。

en: They will growl over it on that day, like the growling of the sea. And if one looks to the land, behold, darkness and distress; and the light is darkened by its clouds.
zh: 到那日，他們要向以色列人咆哮， 像海浪的澎湃聲一樣； 人若望著這地，就只見黑暗與災難； 光在雲中也成了黑暗。
